answer: The Nile River is approximately 7,088 km (4,404 mi) long, making it the longest river in the world. It flows through or borders 11 countries in Africa. The river's basin covers 2,927,843 km².

results.title: Nile - Simple English Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia
results.snippet: The Nile (النيل an-nīl) is a river in Africa. It is the longest river on Earth (about 6,650 km or 4,132 miles), though other rivers carry more water.
